perturbation.original_text: The Evaluation Material will be used by Investor solely in connection with its evaluation of a possible Transaction, and not for any other purpose whatsoever. The Evaluation Material will be kept confidential by Investor in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement and will not be disclosed to any person, except that Investor may disclose the Evaluation Material or portions thereof to those of its employees, officers, directors, partners, members, affiliates, accountants, attorneys, agents, consultants and advisors (these persons being collectively called “ Representatives ”) who (a) need to know such information in order for Investor to be able to evaluate a possible Transaction and (b) have been directed by Investor to treat the Evaluation Material in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Evaluation Material will be used by Investor in connection with its evaluation of a possible Transaction. The Evaluation Material will be kept confidential by Investor and will not be disclosed to any person, except that Investor may disclose the Evaluation Material or portions thereof to those of its employees, officers, directors, partners, members, affiliates, accountants, attorneys, agents, consultants and advisors (these persons being collectively called “ Representatives ”).
perturbation.explanation: By removing "solely" and "not for any other purpose whatsoever", in section 2, creates ambiguity regarding the permitted use of Evaluation Material. This omission contradicts the intention of the NDA, which is to strictly limit its use to the evaluation of the Transaction. It weakens the enforceability of the confidentiality obligation. This change makes the original text from Section 6 more susceptible to interpretation that the material can be used to additional usages other than the evaluation for the transaction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: At any time upon the written request of the Company, Investor will, at its own expense, (i) reasonably promptly redeliver to the Company or, at the at Investor’s option, destroy all copies (including permanently erasing or deleting any electronic copies) of the Evaluation Material in the possession or control of Investor or any of its Representatives and (ii) reasonably promptly destroy all analyses, notes, data, compilations, summaries, forecasts, studies or other documents and materials prepared by Investor or any of its Representatives in connection with their evaluation and review of a possible Transaction that contain, reflect or are based upon or generated from, in whole or in part, any of the Evaluation Material. Upon the Company’s written request, Investor will confirm any such destruction to the Company in writing. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of Evaluation Material, Investor will continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality hereunder respecting Evaluation Material, including without limitation, with respect to oral Evaluation Material and their obligation not to use any Evaluation Material for any purpose other than as specifically permitted in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly limits the Evaluation Material's use to solely evaluating a possible Transaction and for no other purpose. The changed text removes the "solely" and "not for any other purpose whatsoever" limitation, creating ambiguity. This directly contradicts the section stating the Investor's obligation 'not to use any Evaluation Material for any purpose other than as specifically permitted in this Agreement' because the changed text now lacks specificity on permitted uses, weakening the restriction and making the contradiction text from Section 6 no longer applicable in all cases.

perturbation.original_text: Investor shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives. Neither Investor nor any of its Representatives shall, directly or indirectly, (x) market or use (other than as permitted by the Agreement) any Evaluation Material, (y) reproduce or otherwise copy any Evaluation Material except in connection with the Transaction, or (z) disclose, sell, license, offer to sell or license or otherwise transfer or make available any Evaluation Material to any person other than as otherwise permitted by this Agreement. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, Investor will use all reasonable efforts to protect the confidentiality of Evaluation Material, including, without limitation, efforts commensurate with those it employs for the protection of its own confidential and proprietary information.
perturbation.changed_text: Investor shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives. Investor will use reasonable efforts to protect the confidentiality of Evaluation Material, including, without limitation, efforts commensurate with those it employs for the protection of its own confidential and proprietary information.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the explicit prohibitions on marketing, unauthorized use, reproduction, and disclosure of Evaluation Material weakens the NDA's protection of confidential information. This directly contradicts Section 14, which grants the Company the right to seek specific performance and injunctive relief for any breach of the agreement, because with the removal of the list of limitations, there is no explicit breach that can be identified.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Investor hereby agrees that a failure to comply with any provision of this Agreement would cause the Company irreparable harm and that monetary damages would be an inadequate remedy for an actual or threatened breach of this Agreement by Investor or any of its Representatives because of the difficulty of ascertaining the amount of damage that will be suffered by the Company in the event that this Agreement is breached. Accordingly, the Company will be entitled to specific performance of this Agreement and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach without proof of actual damages. Investor further agrees to waive, and to use its reasonable efforts to cause its Representatives to waive, any requirement for the securing or posting of any bond in connection with any such remedy. Any such equitable remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for a breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibited certain actions (marketing, unauthorized use, reproduction, disclosure) regarding Evaluation Material. The changed text removes these explicit prohibitions, only requiring reasonable efforts to protect confidentiality. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (Section 14) which grants the Company the right to specific performance and injunctive relief for any breach of the agreement. The removal of the explicit prohibitions makes it difficult to identify a specific breach that would warrant such relief, creating a strong contradiction and making the clause relating to specific performance potentially unenforceable. As such, according to the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: At any time upon the written request of the Company, Investor will, at its own expense, (i) reasonably promptly redeliver to the Company or, at the at Investor’s option, destroy all copies (including permanently erasing or deleting any electronic copies) of the Evaluation Material in the possession or control of Investor or any of its Representatives and (ii) reasonably promptly destroy all analyses, notes, data, compilations, summaries, forecasts, studies or other documents and materials prepared by Investor or any of its Representatives in connection with their evaluation and review of a possible Transaction that contain, reflect or are based upon or generated from, in whole or in part, any of the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon written request from the Company, Investor will return or destroy all copies of the Evaluation Material as mutually agreed upon.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "at its own expense" as well as "(i) reasonably promptly redeliver to the Company or, at the at Investor’s option, destroy all copies (including permanently erasing or deleting any electronic copies) of the Evaluation Material in the possession or control of Investor or any of its Representatives and (ii) reasonably promptly destroy all analyses, notes, data, compilations, summaries, forecasts, studies or other documents and materials prepared by Investor or any of its Representatives in connection with their evaluation and review of a possible Transaction that contain, reflect or are based upon or generated from, in whole or in part, any of the Evaluation Material", leaves ambiguity in the text. This ambiguity in Section 6 creates conflict with section 14, because there is no explicit description on how to handle or remedy the situation, thus any failure of doing so, can't be identified as a breach of contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Investor hereby agrees that a failure to comply with any provision of this Agreement would cause the Company irreparable harm and that monetary damages would be an inadequate remedy for an actual or threatened breach of this Agreement by Investor or any of its Representatives because of the difficulty of ascertaining the amount of damage that will be suffered by the Company in the event that this Agreement is breached. Accordingly, the Company will be entitled to specific performance of this Agreement and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach without proof of actual damages. Investor further agrees to waive, and to use its reasonable efforts to cause its Representatives to waive, any requirement for the securing or posting of any bond in connection with any such remedy. Any such equitable remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for a breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the investor bears the expense of returning or destroying the evaluation material and provides detailed instructions. The changed text removes the expense clause and simplifies the return/destruction process to 'as mutually agreed upon.' This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text in section 14, because it removes clarity on how the company can enforce the investors' obligations to return/destroy the Evaluation Material. Since section 14 states that a failure to comply with the agreement would cause irreparable harm and be considered a breach of contract, but the changed text lacks sufficient guidelines and parameters, it is therefore ambiguous on what actions could be viewed as non-compliance, resulting in a contradiction and potential dispute over enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of eighteen months from the date of this Agreement, Investor will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, directly or indirectly, solicit for purposes of employment, offer to hire or offer to enter into any contract with any executive officer of the Company with whom Investor has contact, or of whom Investor otherwise becomes aware, during its discussions with the Company or its evaluation of a possible Transaction; provided, however, that this paragraph shall not prohibit Investor from engagement in any general advertising or general solicitation not targeted to the Company’s employees; and provided, further, that Investor shall not be prohibited from soliciting or hiring any such employee whose employment has been terminated by the Company or its affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: Investor will not solicit for purposes of employment any executive officer of the Company without prior written consent.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 7 includes exceptions that allow the Investor to engage in general advertising not specifically targeted at the Company's employees and to solicit/hire employees who have been terminated by the Company. The edited version removes these exceptions, making the non-solicitation clause much broader. This change creates a contradiction because section 16 states that no course of action or failure to act by either party will constitute a waiver by a party of any right or remedy under this Agreement, and no waiver by either party of any right or remedy under this Agreement will be effective unless made in writing, but the new modification in Section 7 makes it a de facto waiver for any other course of action that takes place.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No course of action or failure to act by either party will constitute a waiver by a party of any right or remedy under this Agreement, and no waiver by either party of any right or remedy under this Agreement will be effective unless made in writing.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 7 removes exceptions that were present in the original Section 7. These exceptions allowed the Investor to engage in general advertising not specifically targeted at the Company's employees and to solicit/hire employees who had been terminated by the Company. The removal of these exceptions broadens the non-solicitation clause, contradicting Section 16, which states that no course of action or failure to act by either party will constitute a waiver of any right or remedy under the agreement. The explanation correctly identifies that the new modification in Section 7 effectively creates a de facto waiver for any other course of action that takes place, which is in contradiction with section 16.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the Company and Investor acknowledge and agree that it will not, and it will cause its respective employees, representatives, affiliates and agents not to, publicly disclose, confirm or acknowledge the existence, or terms and conditions, of this Agreement, or the fact that the Company may be considering a possible Transaction with Investor, other than in a press release approved by each party hereto or as otherwise specifically agreed in writing by the parties hereto, except to the extent the Company believes in good faith, after consultation with counsel, it is required by any law, rule or listing requirement to make such disclosure, provided that the Company shall not identify the Investor or any of its affiliates by name without the Investor’s consent.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither the Company nor Investor will publicly disclose the terms of this Agreement without mutual consent.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase "confirm or acknowledge the existence, or terms and conditions, of this Agreement, or the fact that the Company may be considering a possible Transaction with Investor, other than in a press release approved by each party hereto or as otherwise specifically agreed in writing by the parties hereto, except to the extent the Company believes in good faith, after consultation with counsel, it is required by any law, rule or listing requirement to make such disclosure, provided that the Company shall not identify the Investor or any of its affiliates by name without the Investor’s consent.", creates a change that effects section 16. With the change made, section 16 becomes invalid because there is no real description on how to publicly disclose information about the agreement and the limitations, turning into a de facto waiver.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No course of action or failure to act by either party will constitute a waiver by a party of any right or remedy under this Agreement, and no waiver by either party of any right or remedy under this Agreement will be effective unless made in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a detailed explanation of what can and cannot be disclosed, including exceptions for press releases, written agreements, legal requirements, and restrictions on identifying the Investor. The changed text significantly simplifies this, stating that neither party will disclose the terms of the agreement without mutual consent. This omission directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which states that no failure to act by either party will constitute a waiver of any right or remedy unless made in writing. The simplified changed text acts as a de facto waiver of the Investor's rights regarding specific disclosures, because of the omission, creating a strong contradiction.
